items.summary: Sandstone's Series A comes just six months after a Sequoia-led seed round.

items.title: Lovable says it has hit $500M in annualized revenue, with 1 million new projects a week
items.summary: Lovable says it has now surpassed $500 million in annualized run-rate revenue and its users are building businesses and replacing internal software.

items.title: How engineers at Nextdoor use Codex to build without limits
items.summary: How engineers at Nextdoor use Codex with GPT-5.5 to investigate hard-to-reproduce issues, build across platforms, and focus on product outcomes.

items.title: How an e-scooter founder raised $5 million to build space data centers
items.summary: Orbital founder Euwyn Poon built 250,000 scooters at Spin. Now he wants to launch 10,000 space data centers.

items.title: Alex Vindman Survived Trump’s Retaliation Machine. Now He’s Running for Senate
items.summary: In 2019, Alex Vindman testified during President Trump’s first impeachment trial–a decision that ended his military career. Now he wants to challenge the president from the halls of Congress.

items.title: Learning to lead in a hybrid human-AI enterprise
items.summary: As adoption of AI agents looks set to surge by as much as 300% in the next two years, leadership teams are carefully considering the implications of a hybrid human-AI workforce.  Unlike existing enterprise-level automation that relies on manual input

items.title: What Codex unlocks for Notion
items.summary: How Notion uses Codex to one-shot specs, build AI Voice Input for the web, and multiply engineering power across small teams.

items.title: Five things you need to know about AI
items.summary: At SXSW London last week I gave a talk called “Five things you need to know about AI,” in which I shared what I think are the biggest themes in AI right now. I pulled a few things from our first AI10 list, an annual guide to the most important trends

items.title: Why Apple’s slow-and-steady AI bet is starting to look pretty smart
items.summary: Can Apple's new AI glow-up put to bed accusations that it's losing an all-important industry race?
